title: GOOD: connect-per-call for lightwalletd avoids Arc<Mutex> in async closure
description: The payment-send closure (send_fn_box) creates a fresh lightwalletd connection on each call rather than holding a persistent LightwalletdClient. This is documented in a comment: a persistent client would require Arc<Mutex<LightwalletdClient>> because the closure is called from multiple await points in the chat event loop. Arc<Mutex> across await points risks holding the lock across a yield, deadlocking or blocking the executor. Per-call connection trades a short connect latency for clean ownership with no synchronization needed.
close_reason: Review finding acknowledged — existing implementation is correct, no change needed
